person_name: Marius Victorinus, Roman philosopher and Christian convert (4th century)
person_name_full: Marius Victorinus, Roman philosopher and Christian convert (4th century)
notes: Roman rhetorician and philosopher, translator of Plotinus, who converted to Christianity in the 4th century.

docs.title: Correspondence, Book Volume 2
docs.author: Fronto / Marcus Aurelius
docs.excerpt: … ] a man, further, conspicuous for the noblest accomplishments to be thus afflicted by his son's most untimely death, was this in any sense just or fair? If Providence does govern the world, was this too rightly provided?

docs.title: Life of Tyranni Triginta
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … he called Victorinus,⁠ 19 a man of soldierly energy, to share in the imperial power, and in company with him he fought against Gallienus. 2 Having summoned to their aid huge forces of Germans, they protracted the war for a long time, but at last they were conquered.

docs.title: Letter 80
docs.author: Ambrose of Milan
docs.excerpt: Ambrose — if I may speak of myself — a former provincial governor and Roman senator [Ambrose held the rank of consularis before his election as bishop].
